new_classes.definition_en: Water artificially impregnated with carbon dioxide (aerated/carbonated water), not being natural mineral water, classified under CN subheading 2201 10 90 (Chapter 22). Excludes natural mineral water in aerosol containers for skin care (heading 3304).

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the core distinguishing criteria for CN 22011090: (1) it is aerated/carbonated water, (2) it is not natural mineral water (disjoint with NaturalMineralWater), and (3) it is assigned the correct CN subheading code. The legal text also references HS Explanatory Note paragraphs (B) and (C) for heading 2201 which cover mineral waters and aerated waters more broadly — the boundary between 22011011 (natural mineral water, sparkling) and 22011090 (other, including artificially aerated) is partially captured. The exclusion of natural mineral water in aerosol containers for skin care (heading 3304) is noted in the definition but cannot be formally axiomatised as an OWL restriction without a purpose/use property in the current TBox. The reference to HS Explanatory Notes paragraphs (B) and (C) adds interpretative context that cannot be fully formalised in OWL-DL. Hence coverage is 0.75.
